final_answer: ## Complete Solution: Plasmonic-Enhanced Photochemical Decomposition of MB

plan: State the governing formula for total plasmonic-enhanced photochemical decomposition rate: $R_\text{total} = R_0 + \Sigma \cdot \sigma \cdot I$, where $\Sigma \cdot \sigma \cdot I$ is the plasmon-driven charge excitation contribution and $R_0$ is the baseline direct photolysis rate.
Calculate the unenhanced charge excitation rate at each wavelength ($r_\text{base} = \sigma \cdot I$) to establish a reference before applying enhancement.
Apply the electromagnetic enhancement factor $\Sigma$ to obtain the plasmon-enhanced charge excitation rate at each wavelength: $r_\text{enhanced} = \Sigma \cdot \sigma \cdot I$.
Evaluate $R_\text{total}$ at **785 nm** by substituting $\sigma_{785}$, $I$, $\Sigma$, and $R_0^{785}$; note whether $R_0$ is negligible relative to the enhanced term.
Evaluate $R_\text{total}$ at **532 nm** using the same procedure with $\sigma_{532}$ and $R_0^{532}$.
Compute the ratio $R_\text{total}^{785} / R_\text{total}^{532}$ and verify it equals $\sigma_{785}/\sigma_{532}$ when $R_0 \ll \Sigma \cdot \sigma \cdot I$.

Interpret the result physically: explain why the higher charge-excitation cross-section at 785 nm (near-IR, better matched to the broad plasmon resonance) leads to a faster decomposition rate despite the common assumption that shorter wavelengths drive stronger photochemistry.

trajectory.thought: Step 1 establishes the governing formula. The total rate of MB photochemical decomposition in a plasmonic system has two additive contributions:
Step 2 — Unenhanced charge excitation rate at each wavelength.
Computing the ratio and verifying the cross-section dominance.
